import { Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

<Tabs>
<TabItem label="JavaScript (and super languages)" icon="seti:javascript">
:::note
This rule has been implemented but not released yet. It will be available in the next release.
:::
:::caution
This rule is part of the [nursery](/linter/#nursery) group. This means that it is experimental and the behavior can change at any time.
:::
## Summary
- Diagnostic Category: [`lint/nursery/noJsRestrictedProperties`](/reference/diagnostics#diagnostic-category)
- This rule doesn't have a fix.
- The default severity of this rule is [**warning**](/reference/diagnostics#warning).
- Sources: 
  - Same as [`no-restricted-properties`](https://eslint.org/docs/latest/rules/no-restricted-properties)

## How to configure
```json title="biome.json"
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"noJsRestrictedProperties": "error"
			}
		}
	}
}

```
## Description
Disallow specific object properties.

This rule lets you ban property access for exact object/property pairs, all properties on a
given object, or a property name everywhere except for a short allowlist of objects.

It also reports restricted properties when they appear in object destructuring.

This rule requires explicit configuration to specify which properties are restricted, so it does not report anything by default.

## Examples

### Exact object/property restriction

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"noJsRestrictedProperties": {
					"level": "on",
					"options": {
						"entries": [
							{
								"object": "require",
								"property": "ensure",
								"message": "Use dynamic import() instead."
							}
						]
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

In this example, the rule reports the access of the `ensure` property on the `require` object, and emits the message "Use dynamic import() instead.":

```js
require.ensure("./entry")
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text">code-block.js:1:9 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-js-restricted-properties">lint/nursery/noJsRestrictedProperties</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: Orange;">⚠</span></strong> <span style="color: Orange;">Do not use 'require.ensure'.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>1 │ </strong>require.ensure(&quot;./entry&quot;)<br />   <strong>   │ </strong>        <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>2 │ </strong><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Use dynamic import() instead.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Remove this usage to comply with the project's guidelines.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery">https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

### Property-wide restriction with an allowlist

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"noJsRestrictedProperties": {
					"level": "on",
					"options": {
						"entries": [
							{
								"property": "__defineGetter__",
								"message": "Use Object.defineProperty() instead.",
								"allowObjects": [
									"Object"
								]
							}
						]
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

In this example, the rule reports any access to the property `__defineGetter__`, except for `Object` object, and it emits the message "Use Object.defineProperty() instead.":

```js
foo.__defineGetter__
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text">code-block.js:1:5 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-js-restricted-properties">lint/nursery/noJsRestrictedProperties</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: Orange;">⚠</span></strong> <span style="color: Orange;">Do not use property '&#95;&#95;defineGetter&#95;&#95;'.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>1 │ </strong>foo.&#95;&#95;defineGetter&#95;&#95;<br />   <strong>   │ </strong>    <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>2 │ </strong><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Use Object.defineProperty() instead.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Property '&#95;&#95;defineGetter&#95;&#95;' is only allowed on these objects: Object.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Remove this usage to comply with the project's guidelines.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery">https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

```js
Object.__defineGetter__
```

### Object-wide restriction with allowed exceptions

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"noJsRestrictedProperties": {
					"level": "on",
					"options": {
						"entries": [
							{
								"object": "arguments",
								"message": "Avoid accessing arbitrary arguments properties.",
								"allowProperties": [
									"length"
								]
							}
						]
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

In the following example, when the rule encounters the object `arguments`, it reports all properties except for `length` with the message "Avoid accessing arbitrary arguments properties.":

```js
arguments.callee
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text">code-block.js:1:11 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-js-restricted-properties">lint/nursery/noJsRestrictedProperties</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: Orange;">⚠</span></strong> <span style="color: Orange;">Do not access properties on 'arguments'.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>1 │ </strong>arguments.callee<br />   <strong>   │ </strong>          <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>2 │ </strong><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Avoid accessing arbitrary arguments properties.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Only these properties are allowed on 'arguments': length.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Remove this usage to comply with the project's guidelines.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery">https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

```js
arguments.length
```

## Options

### `entries`

An array of restricted object/property combinations. Depending on the provided options, each entry can:

- Restrict a specific property on a specific object.
- Restrict all properties on a specific object except for an allowlist of properties.
- Restrict a specific property everywhere except for an allowlist of objects.
- Provide a custom message to include in the diagnostic when the restriction is violated.

### `entries[].object`

The object for which the restriction applies. If not provided, the restriction applies to all objects. If combined with `property`, it restricts only that property on the specified object.

### `entries[].property`

The property for which the restriction applies. If not provided, the restriction applies to all properties. If combined with `object`, it restricts that property only on that object.

### `entries[].allowObjects`

When restricting a property, an optional allowlist of objects that are exempt from the restriction. Only applicable when `property` is provided and `object` is not provided.

It conflicts with `allowProperties`.

### `entries[].allowProperties`

When restricting an object, an optional allowlist of properties that are exempt from the restriction. Only applicable when `object` is provided and `property` is not provided.

It conflicts with `allowObjects`.

### `entries[].message`

An optional custom message to include in the diagnostic when this restriction is violated. If not provided, a default message will be used.

Use this to give context and explain why the property is restricted, and to suggest an alternative.
